title: Thelypteris bulusanica
intro: Thelypteris bulusanica is a species of fern native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae and commonly known as Bulusan fern. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris bulusanica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are tea infusions and extracts, which are often used to relieve colds, sore throats, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Thelypteris bulusanica is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to the Philippines and is found in the Bicol Region. This species is classified within the genus Thelypteris, which includes a wide range of ferns. The Thelypteris genus is part of the larger group of Thelypteridaceae, a family of deciduous and evergreen ferns. The classification of Thelypteris bulusanica is further refined using binomial nomenclature, with the species name bulusanica referring to its specific characteristics.
